mnemonics: Binary Logic is like a Light Switch: On (1) or Off (0) - no in-between.
keyPoints: Binary logic uses two discrete values: 1 (TRUE/ON) and 0 (FALSE/OFF).
The three fundamental logic operations are AND, OR, and NOT.
These gates are the building blocks for all digital circuits, from simple adders to complex processors.
commonMistakes: Confusing the output of an AND gate (only high if ALL inputs are high) with an OR gate (high if ANY input is high).
Forgetting that a NOT gate (inverter) only has one input and simply flips its value.
